provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Budget Regulations, 2015.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Budget Act, 2015; “ sectors ” includes real, fiscal, external and monetary sectors; “ budget committee ” means a committee established pursuant to section 18 of the Act ; “ Contingency Fund ” means the contingent fund referred to in section 36 of the Act ; “ devolving funds to local government ” means and shall include recurrent block grants composed of specific allocations for Other Charges, subventions including basket funds and development grants including local and foreign; “ domestic borrowing ” means borrowing from domestic sources including the issuance of government securities; “ formula ” means a criteria for allocation of resources to local government authorities and includes but not limited to population, size of the area, road network, number of villages and number of poor residents; “ Gross Domestic Product ” means total value of goods and services produced by residents of a country in a given period of time normally in one year; “ macro-economic assumptions ” means and includes stability of the world macro-economic variables such as world Gross Domestic Product and inflation, good weather, peace and harmony of the country and the neighbouring countries; “ Public Investment and Management Operational Manual ” means a guideline for selection of a new project that shall be included in the next financial year budget; “ Public Expenditure Review ” is a consultative process between government and stakeholders where allocation and management of public spending is analyzed. Part II – Macroeconomic and fiscal framework

provisions.title: Principles of fiscal responsibility
provisions.content: (1) Where preparing policy objectives, the Government and public entities shall adhere to principles of fiscal responsibility. (2) Principles of fiscal responsibility referred to under sub regulation (1) shall seek to ensure that: (a) borrowing conforms with the National Debt Management Policy, Medium Term Debt Management Strategy, the Loans, Guarantees and Grants Act ; [Cap.134] (b) fiscal policies shall promote investments and growth as stipulated in the Plan and Budget Guidelines and Five Year Development Plan; (c) guaranteed loans, pension obligations and pending bills conform to the National Debt Management Policy, Medium Term Debt Management Strategy and Loans, Guarantees and Grants Act ; [Cap.134] (d) wage bill shall be covered in the recurrent expenditure and financed by domestic revenues; and (e) budget preparation and execution processes shall be participatory involving key stakeholders with the aim of increasing transparency and accountability.

provisions.title: Deviation from the principles of prudent fiscal management
provisions.content: (1) The Minister may deviate from principles of prudent fiscal management where events of force majeure occur and which cannot be funded from the Contingency Fund or other funding mechanisms provided for under the Act . (2) Event constituting force majeure shall include: (a) world economic slump; (b) unfavourable climatic conditions; (c) state of emergence, including war; (d) natural calamities such as earthquake; and (e) outbreak of diseases. (3) The Minister shall publish a report on any deviation and present to the next session of the National Assembly.

provisions.title: Macroeconomic and fiscal forecasts, financial programming and macroeconimic modeling
provisions.content: (1) The Minister shall form the National Financial Programming Working Group which shall be responsible for financial programming and macroeconomic modeling. (2) The National Financial Programming Working Group shall be comprise of representative from: (a) the Ministry of Finance who shall be the Chairman; (b) the Planning Commission; (c) the Bank of Tanzania; (d) the Tanzania Revenue Authority; and (e) the National Bureau of Statistics. (3) The Minister may co-opt any person who may be of assistance during deliberations of the National Financial. (4) The National Financial Programming Working Group shall meet quarterly in each financial year to review, reconcile and forecast major macroeconomic variables of the four sectors . (5) The National Financial Programming Working Group shall use Financial Programming and Macroeconomic Modeling as a tool for making reconciliation and forecast of the four blocks. (6) The National Financial Programming Working Group shall use the Financial Programming and Macroeconomic Modeling for purposes of: (a) assessing the world economic dynamics and its impacts on the national economy through analyzing different reports indicating performance of the first world economies, emerging economies and the economies of the sub-Saharan countries; (b) conducting debt sustainability analysis annually, and prepare macroeconomic risk analysis report; (c) analyzing economic developments achieved on each block and challenges; (d) preparing macroeconomic outlook for the four sectors for the coming financial year based on macroeconomic assumptions; and (e) preparing medium term macroeconomic framework that is used for the preparation of the Plans and Budget Guidelines and National Budget documents.

provisions.title: Coordination with national development plan
provisions.content: (1) The coordination of national development plan shall be as stipulated under section 7 of the Act on assumption that the operation of development and recurrent budgets are based on the following criteria: (a) commitments for expenditures and payment are made as per approved budget; (b) disbursements of public moneys for development (c) submission of quarterly expenditure performance reports to the Ministry responsible for finance and the Planning Commission by 15 th day of every next month, which shall be a basis for disbursement of funds for next quarter; (d) payment of personnel emoluments is made directly to employees' bank accounts; (e) procurement of goods and services comply with the National Procurement Policy and the Public Procurement Act , to ensure quality and value for money. (2) The Planning Commission shall scrutinize, analyze and approve new development projects prior to their inclusion in the budget. (3) The criteria for new projects shall be: (a) aligned to the national plans’ priorities as stipulated in the Public Investment and Management Operational Manual ; (b) strategic outcomes of the project based on specific strategic goals such as equity and regional development, skills and technological gains. (4) The private sector shall be involved in financing and implementing major development projects through public private partnership as stipulated under the Public Private Partnership Policy and Public Private Partnership Act . (5) Allocation of funds to the local government authorities shall be based on the following: (a) approved Local Government Authorities Strategic Plans; (b) formula based allocation criteria for other charges and development budget; and (c) annual performance assessment of local government, including reports from the Controller and Auditor-General. (6) All local government authorities shall be graded for purpose of planning and allocation or resources. (7) Priority for allocating public moneys for investing in the development projects shall base on the following criteria: (a) strategic nature of the project; (b) on-going projects; and (c) sector wise approach and basket funds received from development partners. Part III – Management of the budget process

provisions.title: Public Expenditure Review
provisions.content: (1) The Minister shall ensure that members of public participate in the national budget process through the following fora: (a) the Public Expenditure Review ; and (b) the Task Force on Tax Reforms. (2) The objective of the Public Expenditure Review forum shall be to analyze the extent to which policy priorities are practically implemented through budget allocation in order to increase the effectiveness and efficiency of public spending.

provisions.title: Functions of Public Expenditure Review Forum
provisions.content: (1) The functions of the Public Expenditure Review forum shall be to: (a) conduct analytical studies which inform the government and public management process; and (b) furnish findings and recommendations from studies to the Plan and Budget Guidelines Committee for policy formulation as part of government priorities for implementation in the medium term. (2) Public expenditure review shall be a continuous process culminates in the national policy dialogue week in the month of September to October for each financial year.

provisions.title: Composition of Public Expenditure Review Forum
provisions.content: (1) There shall be the Extended Public Expenditure Review Champion Group constituting of the following members; (a) the Permanent Secretary to the Treasury who shall be the Chairman; (b) the Permanent Secretary - Prime Minister’s Office; (c) the Executive Secretary of the Planning Commission; (d) the Governor of the Bank of Tanzania; (e) the International Monetary Fund Country Representative; (f) the General Budget Support Chairman; (g) the European Union Ambassador; (h) the United Nation's Representative; (i) a representative of the Civil Societies Organizations under Policy Forum; and (j) a representative of the Tanzania Private Sector Foundation. (2) The Commissioner for Budget shall provide Secretariat to the Extended Public Expenditure Review Champion Group.

provisions.title: Objective of the Task Force on Tax Reforms
provisions.content: (1) The objective of the Task Force on Tax Reforms forum shall be to receive public opinion and deliberate on proposals on tax policy implementation and revenue measures for the next financial year. (2) The process of receiving opinion and proposals from the public shall include: (a) advertising through media and Ministry of Finance website by early December each year calling for the public to submit proposals and opinion on taxes and non-tax measures to be considered for reforms in the subsequent financial year; (b) submitting of proposals and recommendations to the Permanent Secretary from January to February for each financial year; (c) discussing submitted proposals by members effectively from March to April each year and make recommendations to the Minister by early May each financial year; and (d) deliberating on public opinion and proposals on tax policy implementation and revenue measures are submitted to the advisory committee for review.

provisions.title: Composition of the Task Force on Tax Reforms
provisions.content: The Task Force on Tax Reforms shall be composed of: (a) the Permanent Secretary to the Treasury; (b) the Deputy Permanent Secretaries to the Treasury; (c) the Commissioner for Policy Analysis; (d) the Commissioner for Budget; (e) the Director of Legal Services; (f) the Assistant Commissioners for Policy Analysis; (g) the Chief Parliamentary Draftsman; (h) the Treasury Registrar; (i) a representative of the Planning Commission; (j) a representative from the Regional Administration and Local Government Authorities; (k) a representative from Ministry responsible for Lands; (l) a representative from the Ministry responsible for natural resources and tourism; (m) a representative from the Ministry responsible for East African Co-operation; (n) a representative from the Ministry responsible for agriculture, food security and co-operatives; (o) a representative from the Tanzania Revenue Authority; (p) a representative from the Joint Finance Commission; (q) a representative from the Procurement and Supplies Professionals and Technicians Board; (r) a representative from the Confederation of Tanzania Industries; (s) a representative from the Tanzania Chambers of Commerce Industries and Agriculture; (t) a representative from the Tanzania Private Sector Foundation; (u) a representative from the Tanzania Accountants’ Association; (v) a representative from the Agricultural Council of Tanzania; and (w) any other member co-opted from time to time as the Chairman may deem necessary.

provisions.title: Powers and functions of the Paymaster General
provisions.content: (1) The functions of the Paymaster General shall be: (a) to advise the Minister on the preparation of— (i) the Plan and Budget Guidelines; (ii) revenue measures; (iii) budget estimates of the government; (iv) budget policy highlights and new revenue measures to be announced by the Minister; (v) the Appropriation Bill, the Finance Bill and subsidiary legislation(s) required to implement the Government’s budgetary proposals, (vi) budget execution reports; (vii) preparation and submission of mid year review for Cabinet approval; (viii) annual cash flow plan based on work plans, procurements plans and recruitment plans as per approved budget; (ix) any other budget documents of which the Minister is required to submit to the National Assembly; (b) require the accounting officers to submit quarterly expenditure commitments reports within thirty days after the end of every quarter for the preparation of a consolidated report; (c) require the accounting officers to prepare and submit reallocation made within respective votes; (d) monitor any person who is vested with authority for collection and management of public revenues to adhere to the principles for management of revenues as stipulated under the Act ; (e) require any person who is vested with authority for collection of public revenues to prepare and submit report on collected public revenues; (f) require accounting officers to submit any information that he considers necessary for the implementation of the Act and this Regulations. (2) The Internal Auditor General shall monitor the budget through budget audit in order to ensure compliance with the Plan and Budget Guidelines, Treasury circular and other directives, and advise the Paymaster General accordingly. (3) Any information required under this Regulation shall be made in writing and comply with the specified deadline.

provisions.title: Mechanism for conducting periodic review
provisions.content: The Government Budget Division shall, in collaboration with the Policy Analysis Division and Treasury Registrar— (a) conduct periodic review of revenues of public entities with a view to ensure that revenue collections are performed in the most efficient and effective manner; (b) conduct periodic review of expenditures of public entities with a view to ensure that expenditures are in line with the institutional and national goals and objectives; and (c) have unrestricted access of information on revenues and expenditures of public entities in a specified format.

provisions.title: Functions of the Planning Commission
provisions.content: (1) Further to stipulated under the Act , the Planning Commission shall assess the state of the national resources for development and advise the Government on the efficient utilization of those resources by: (a) conducting research and analyze country’s resources potentials in order to speed up the provision of the basic conditions of broad based economic growth and poverty reduction; (b) preparing Medium and Long Term Development Plans; (c) preparing annual development plan focusing on advising the Government to strategic investment for unleashing growth in infrastructure development, agriculture, industry, human resources development, tourism, trade and financial services; (d) advising the Government in investing and improving social services which include provision of clean and safe water, improvement of health services, and provision of quality education at all levels; (e) advising on efficient measures of improving resources mobilization and collection strategies; (f) advising on the measures of scaling up the role and participation of private sector in economic growth by improving business environment; (g) preparing annual national economic survey report for analyzing socio-economic achievements and challenges of economic status of a country; and (h) conduct monitoring and evaluation on implementation of the National Development Plan regularly in collaboration with Ministry of Finance, implementing sectors and submit a report semiannually to the Cabinet for approval.

provisions.title: Powers and functions of Commissioner for Budget
provisions.content: (1) The Commissioner for Budget shall— (a) monitor the implementation of budget through scrutiny of progress reports submitted by the Government and public entities and take necessary measures to address any anomalies; (b) undertake periodic public expenditure tracking and projects inspection activities; and (c) advise the Paymaster General on budgetary commitments of the Government and decisions having financial obligations in respect of Other Charges, Personnel Emoluments and Development Budget of the Government for consideration in the budget management process.

provisions.title: Composition of the National Plan and Budget Guidelines Committee
provisions.content: (1) The National Plan and Budget Guidelines Committee shall be composed of: (a) the Deputy Executive Secretary of the Planning Commission; (b) the Commissioner for Budget; (c) the Commissioner for Policy Analysis; (d) the Commissioner for External Finance; (e) the Commissioner for Public Private Partnership; (f) Assistant Commissioners for Budget; (g) Assistant Commissioners for Policy Analysis; (h) Other Deputy Executive Secretaries Planning Commission; (i) Coordinator from Reforms Coordinating Unit, President’s Office, State House; (j) the Director for Coordination of Government Business from Prime Minister’s Office; (k) the Director of Policy and Planning from Prime Minister’s Office, Regional Administration and Local Governments; (l) the Director of Policy Development from President’s Office, Public Service Management; (m) the Accountant General; (n) the Treasury Registrar; (o) a representative from Bank of Tanzania; (p) a representative from Tanzania Revenue Authority; and (q) any other members to be co-opted from time to time by Permanent Secretary as and when necessary. (2) The Deputy Executive Secretary Planning Commission shall be the chairman of the committee and shall preside at all meetings of the committee and the Commissioner for Budget shall be the vice-chairman. (3) The Commissioner for Budget shall form a secretariat to the National Plan and Budget Guidelines Committee.

provisions.title: The powers and functions of Accounting officers
provisions.content: (1) Accounting officers shall each establish budget committees for shall be the Chairman which the accounting officer. (2) The composition of the budget committees shall be as follows— (a) at Ministerial level: (i) Permanent secretary shall be the chairman; (ii) Director of Planning and Policy shall be the secretary; and (iii) Heads of department, section and unit shall be members. (b) at Independent Departments Level: (i) Head of Independent Department shall be the chairman; (ii) Head of Planning shall be the secretary; and (iii) Heads of department, section and unit shall be members. (c) at Regional Secretariats level: (i) Regional Administrative Secretary shall be the chairman; (ii) Head of Planning and Coordination department shall be the secretary; and (iii) Heads of department, section and unit shall be members. (d) at Local Governments level: (i) Local Government Authority’s Director shall be the chairman; (ii) Local Government Authority's Head of Planning shall be the secretary; and (iii) Heads of department, section and unit shall be members. (e) in respect of other public entities: (i) Chief Executive Officers shall be the chairman; (ii) Head of Planning as secretary; and (iii) Heads of department, section and unit shall be members. (3) The functions of the budget committees shall be to— (a) review revenue collection measures; (b) allocate resources based on strategic plan and national priorities without exceeding the ceiling provided by the Paymaster General; (c) evaluate budget performance; and (d) perform other functions as described in the Plan and Budget Guidelines, Treasury Registrars circulars and directives. Part IV – Budget preparation and approval process

provisions.title: Stages in the budget process
provisions.content: (1) The stages in the budget process shall be as stipulated in the Act . (2) For the purpose of subregulation (1) , there shall be an Advisory Committee on tax measures composed of the following members: (a) the Minister; (b) the Minister responsible for Finance from the Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar; (c) Deputy Ministers responsible for Finance; (d) the Permanent Secretary; (e) the Chief Parliamentary Draftsman; (f) the Principal Secretary Ministry responsible for Finance from Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar; (g) Deputy Permanent Secretaries responsible for Treasury; (h) the Executive Secretary, President’s Office Planning Commission; (i) the Governor of the Bank of Tanzania; (j) the Commissioner General of Tanzania Revenue Authority; (k) the Executive Director of the Tanzania Investment Centre; (l) the Executive Director of Tanzania Chamber of Commerce, Industries and Agriculture; (m) the Head of Department of Economics from the University of Dar es Salaam; (n) the Director General of the Tanzania Communication Regulatory Authority; (o) the Executive Director of the Tanzania Private Sector Foundation; (p) the Executive Director of the Research on Poverty Alleviation; (q) the Executive Director of the Economic Social Research Foundation; (r) the Executive Director of the National Board of Accountant and Auditors; (s) the Chief Executive Officer of the Confederation of Tanzania Industries; and (t) the Principal of the Institute of Finance Management. (3) The Advisory Committee may co-opt any person who may be of assistance to the committee. (4) The Commissioner for Policy Analysis shall be a Secretary to the Advisory Committee. (5) The functions of Advisory Committee shall be to deliberate on tax policy implementation and revenue measures as submitted by the Task Force on Tax Reforms.

provisions.title: Submission of other budget documents
provisions.content: The nature of information to be presented in the budget estimates and the form of presentation of budget documents shall be as described in the Plan and Budget Guidelines.

provisions.title: Carryover of unspent disbursed funds
provisions.content: (1) All carryovers of unspent funds shall require prior approval of the Paymaster General. (2) Funds disbursed but not spent in a particular financial year may be rolled over to a subsequent year subject to the following conditions: (a) capital expenditure: unspent funds on capital expenditure may only be rolled over to finalize projects still in progress or for other capital purposes; (b) transfer payments: savings on transfer payments may not be rolled over for purposes other than originally voted for; and (c) current expenditure: a maximum of five per cent of a Vote’s current expenditure in the main estimate of expenditure may be rolled over.

provisions.title: Submission of carry over
provisions.content: (1) Accounting officers shall submit carry over requests in form of a statement of un-discharged commitments fifteen days before 30 th June of each financial year to the Paymaster General with copies to Controller and Auditor General showing: (a) reasons for under spending; (b) commitments that the funds rolled over shall be spent; and (c) an assessment of spending capacity taking into consideration of the new budget allocation. (2) All un-discharged commitments at the end of any financial year shall be spent within the first quarter of the subsequent financial year. (3) All unspent carryovers shall be returned to the Consolidated Fund after the end of the first quarter of the subsequent financial year. (4) The Paymaster General may reject all or part of a request for a rollover where the requests do not conform to the conditions under sub regulation (1) . Part V – Contingency Fund

provisions.title: Minister to administer the Contingency Fund
provisions.content: The Contingency Fund shall be one per cent of the total budget set aside at the commencement of each financial year and shall to be administered by the Minister.

provisions.title: Administration of the Contingency Fund
provisions.content: (1) The Contingency Fund shall be administered and managed within the Treasury's governance framework. (2) For the purpose of subregulation (1) , the Paymaster General shall appoint the Accounting Officer of the Contingency Fund . (3) The Minister shall be responsible to National Assembly on all policies, decisions and actions on the use of the Contingency Fund . (4) Accounting Officer shall apply to the Paymaster General for approval to withdraw money from the Contingency Fund .

provisions.title: Contigency Fund Committee Members
provisions.content: (1) There shall be a Contingency Fund Committee which shall provide technical support the Paymaster General and the Accounting Officer for managing the Contingency Fund . (2) The Contingency Fund Committee shall comprise of the following members: (a) the Commissioner for Budget; (b) the Commissioner for Policy Analysis; (c) the Accountant General; and (d) the Internal Auditor General

provisions.title: Withdraw money from the fund
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall timely inform the Paymaster General on funds requirements for withdrawal of money from the Contingency Fund . (2) Once an advance has been made, it shall be the responsibility of the receiving Accounting Officer to ensure that the money is used only for the purpose for which it was advanced. (3) The Paymaster General shall make follow up on the use of the funds, including periodic monitoring on the use of the advanced money. (4) Accounting officers seeking advances from the Contingency Fund shall be required to comply with financial rules, regulations and procedures as stipulated in the Public Finance Act . [Cap. 348] (5) The Paymaster General shall certify on the existence of unforeseen event before approval of payment from the Contingency Fund .

provisions.title: Reallocation of funds
provisions.content: A request for reallocation of funds may be considered after mid year review of the budget implementation.

provisions.title: Reallocation within votes
provisions.content: (1) An accounting officer may reallocate funds within a vote up to seven per cent of the total budget allocation for the respective vote. (2) Any reallocation exceeding seven per cent shall require approval of the Minister. (3) Total reallocation within a vote shall not exceed ten percent of the total budget allocation for the respective vote.

provisions.title: Reallocation between votes
provisions.content: (1) All reallocations between votes shall require approval of the Minister. (2) Reallocations between votes shall not exceed five percent of the total government budget. (3) Reallocations shall not be made if— (a) it is likely to may result into future liabilities; (b) it relates to reallocations of funds for capital or development projects without the approval from Executive Secretary of the Planning Commission; (c) it relates to wages and debt servicing without the approval of the Minister; or (d) it relates to reallocations of funds from core activities to non-core activities. (4) Accounting officers shall submit to the Minister a report on reallocations made within a vote on quarterly basis. Part VI – Budget execution, monitoring, evaluation and reporting

provisions.title: In year reporting
provisions.content: (1) The Accountant General shall submit to the Paymaster General information in respect of every government and public entities on actual revenues and expenditure of the previous month. (2) Public entities, special funds and public social security funds shall submit reports to the Paymaster General on actual revenues and expenditures on quarterly basis. (3) The report or information referred to under subregulations (1) and (2) shall be submitted within thirty days after the end of the respective quarter. (4) The Commissioner for Budget shall, in collaboration with the Commissioner for Policy Analysis, issue and publish the quarterly budget execution report within thirty days after the end of the respective quarter. (5) The Accounting officers shall submit quarterly progress reports to the Paymaster General within thirty days after the end of the respective quarter. Part VII – Management of revenues

provisions.title: Revenue collection proposal
provisions.content: (1) The procedure for receiving revenue proposals from stakeholders on taxes and non-tax revenues to be considered for reforms for the next fiscal year shall be published in the local media by early December each year. (2) The publication shall call upon members of the general public, Members of Parliament, academic institutions, non-governmental organizations, faith based organizations and others to submit proposals and recommendations to the Paymaster General between months of January and February for each financial year. (3) The proposals and recommendations received shall be submitted to the Task Force on Tax Reforms by end of February of each financial year. (4) The Task Force on Tax Reforms shall effectively discuss proposals and recommendations received from March to April each year and make recommendations to the Minister by early May of each financial year. (5) The Minister shall submit the recommendation of the Tax Force on Tax Reform to the Advisory Committee soon upon receiving such recommendations. (6) The Advisory Committee shall advice the Minister on the new revenue measures by May of each financial year. (7) There shall be pre budget meeting of Sectoral Council of Ministers of Finance for East Africa Community by early April of each financial year. (8) There shall be meeting of Sectoral Council of Ministers of Finance and Trade for East Africa Community by early June of each financial year. Part VIII – Oversight of public entities and extra-budgetary agencies

provisions.title: Extra budgetary agencies
provisions.content: Extra-budgetary agencies shall— (a) prepare budgets based on the Medium Term Expenditure Framework format as provided under the Plan and Budget Guidelines; and (b) submit to the parent Ministries prior to submitting to the Minister for scrutiny by February each financial year.

provisions.title: Transitional provisions
provisions.content: (1) The Minister shall submit to the National Assembly a report of votes that have been receiving consecutively qualified audit report for the past three years. (2) Votes having not complied with any provisions in the Act , the National Assembly may limit amounts approved to eighty five per cent of their respective budgets. (3) The penalty under subregulation (2) , shall not limit other disciplinary penalty as stipulated in the Act. the contingent fund referred to in section 36 of the Act

definitions.term: Public Investment and Management Operational Manual
definitions.definition: a guideline for selection of a new project that shall be included in the next financial year budget
